channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Fear, disease and debt afflict Venezuelans released from prison after post-election arrests
item.description: TOCUYITO, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s President Nicolás Maduro labeled them as terrorists on national television. They were plucked from pharmacies, apartment buildings and other locations, and thrown in prison for months.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After the initial ecstasy of freedom, released Israeli hostages face a long road to recovery
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — When Ilana Gritzewsky returned to Israel after being held captive in Gaza for 55 days in November 2023, she had so much adrenaline coursing through her body she couldn’t sleep for two days. “You don’t understand that it’s really over," Gritzewsky recalled.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo say they plan to take their fight to the capital
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels who captured eastern Congo's largest city said Thursday they want to take their fight to the far-off capital, Kinshasa, while Congo’s president called for a massive military mobilization to resist the rebellion and his defense minister rejected calls for talks
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama's president says there will be no negotiation about ownership of canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama President José Raúl Mulino said Thursday there will be no negotiation with the United States over ownership of the Panama Canal, and he hopes U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France hands over last military base in Chad
item.description: N’DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — France handed over its last military base in Chad to local authorities on Thursday, two months after the former French colony in central Africa broke off its defense cooperation agreement with Paris.
item.dc:creator: MOUTA ALI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moment IDF soldier is reunited with family seen on video
item.description: Agam Berger, an IDF soldier who was taken hostage during Hamas' attacks on Oct. 7, 2023, is reunited with her family after spending 482 days in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More schools in France are getting special mailboxes for children to flag abuse
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A special mailbox for children to flag abuses was set up Thursday at a Paris primary school, a first in the French capital as part of nationwide effort to encourage more victims of sexual violence to speak up.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Russian drone strikes an apartment building in Ukraine, killing at least 6
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian drone blasted a hole in an apartment building in northeastern Ukraine during a nighttime attack, killing at least six people and wounding nine others, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Albanians pay last respects to Orthodox Christian Archbishop Anastasios
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Thousands of Albanians gathered in the capital on Thursday to pay their last respects to Archbishop Anastasios, who revived the country’s Orthodox Christian Church after the fall of the Communist party in 1990.
item.dc:creator: BY LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chaos and uncertainty swirl around Trump's foreign aid freeze
item.description: President Trump’s decision to clean house at the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) and initially freeze all foreign assistance is fueling chaos and uncertainty in Washington and across the world.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: International Criminal Court has Putin, Netanyahu in its sights, yet its courtrooms are empty
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — For a few hours last week, the International Criminal Court looked poised to take a Libyan warlord into custody. Instead, member state Italy sent the head of a notorious network of detention centers back home.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Investigators recover black boxes from plane in DC crash
item.description: There were 60 passengers and four crew members aboard the plane, which was arriving from Wichita, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Australia wakes to more antisemitic graffiti after a discovery of explosives was revealed by police
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Three more incidents of antisemitic graffiti were found across Sydney on Thursday morning, leading Australian political leaders to warn of an escalation in hatred and decry as terrorism explosives found earlier in a trailer on the city’s outskirts.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels take more towns as they expand control after seizing east Congo's largest city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rebels backed by Rwanda captured more towns in eastern Congo on Wednesday as they moved beyond the key city of Goma in an apparent attempt to expand their control in the conflict-battered region.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leader of rebels who toppled Syrian President Bashar Assad is named country's interim president
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The Syrian factions that toppled President Bashar Assad last month named an Islamist former rebel leader as the country‘s interim president on Wednesday in a push to project a united front as they face the monumental task of rebuilding Syria after nearly 14 years of civil war.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republicans urge Trump to sanction ICC after failed legislation push
item.description: Senate Republicans are recommending President Trump issue an executive order to sanction the International Criminal Court (ICC) for its pursuit of war crimes cases against Israel, after Senate Democrats blocked sanctions legislation absent a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Backlash may shatter President Macron's plans to leave mark on Notre Dame windows
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron’s plans for installing contemporary windows inside the restored Notre Dame Cathedral is facing furious opposition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage details horrors of captivity, credits kidnapped IDF soldier with saving her life
item.description: Former hostage Amit Soussana, who was the first Israeli woman to speak about being sexual assaulted while in Hamas captivity, says kidnapped IDF soldier Liri Albag saved her life.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 30 dead in stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India, police say
item.description: At least 30 people were killed and another 60 injured in a stampede at the Maha Kumbh festival, a six-week Hindu festival and the world’s largest religious gathering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India's Modi speaks with 'dear friend' President Trump amid hopes of furthering ties
item.description: Following a call between President Donald Trump and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, India is seeking to boost bilateral ties and trade with the U.S. amid an already friendly relationship between the two leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Year of the Snake is underway with festivities around the world
item.description: The holiday — known as the Spring Festival in China, Tet in Vietnam and Seollal in Korea — is a major festival celebrated around the world.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Denmark to increase military spending in North Atlantic by $2 billion amid row with Trump over Greenland
item.description: Denmark is increasing military spending in the North Atlantic amid President Donald Trump’s bid to have Greenland sold or ceded to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump invites Israel's Netanyahu to meet with him at the White House next week
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — President Donald Trump has invited Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House next week as the first foreign leader to visit in Trump's second term, Netanhayu and the White House said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US ships return to Red Sea amid Gaza ceasefire
item.description: U.S. ships are returning to the Red Sea following promises from Yemen’s Houthi rebels to abstain from attacks on American and British vessels. The pledge, which comes after more than a year of shipping traffic disruption on a major international trade route, was made on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Israeli President Herzog highlights antisemitism in UN speech as new report shows shocking trend
item.description: Israel's President Isaac Herzog addressed the U.N. on Monday at its annual Holocaust Remembrance Day event. His speech comes as a new report shows a worrying rise in antisemitic attitudes globally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage Naama Levy breaks silence in first message since her release
item.description: Naama Levy, one of the four IDF soldiers released from Hamas captivity on Saturday, writes a message of gratitude in her first statement since arriving home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US consulate warns of gun battles, IEDs, kidnappings in Mexican border towns near Texas
item.description: U.S. officials have put several Mexican border towns near Texas under the highest-level travel advisory amid gun battles, kidnappings and IEDs in the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South African elephant kills tourist who was trying to save children at Kruger National Park
item.description: A tourist at South Africa's Kruger National Park was trampled to death by an elephant as he was trying to protect children, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A weakened Iran has Iraq looking to curb Tehran-backed extremists in country
item.description: The Iraqi government is looking to gain control of Iranian-aligned armed groups that operate inside of Iraq but outside state control, in yet another sign of Iran's weakened position since October 7.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, WGN 9

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 22 reported killed in Lebanon before agreement to extend deadline for Israeli forces to withdraw
item.description: MAYS AL-JABAL, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli forces in southern Lebanon on Sunday opened fire on protesters demanding their withdrawal in line with a ceasefire agreement, killing at least 22 and injuring 124, Lebanese health officials reported.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Romanian prehistoric gold treasures stolen from Dutch museum
item.description: The Geto-Dacian artifacts belonged to the National History Museum in the capital Bucharest and were on loan to the Drents Museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: CIA believes COVID most likely originated from lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory, according to an assessment released Saturday that points the finger at China even while acknowledging that the spy agency has “low confidence” in its own conclusion.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, WFLA, NBC
